title: hash_sz try_from in sign/verify is unreachable dead code
description: In key.rs::sign() and key.rs::verify(), after validating hash.len() == 32, the code does: let hash_sz = std::ffi::c_int::try_from(hash.len()).map_err(|_| Error::InvalidArg(...))?; The error branch is provably unreachable (32 fits in c_int; the comment even says so). The try_from is noise that implies a runtime check without providing one (clippy::useless_conversion). Idiomatic Rust: let hash_sz: c_int = 32; or hash.len() as c_int with a comment.
